content: Inbox uses a pending/done directory structure: new messages land in .pi/inbox/pending/, and the processor moves them to .pi/inbox/done/ after handling. Directory location serves as processing status — no 'processed' flag needed. Files are named {timestamp}-{uuid}.jsonl for natural time ordering. Processing is atomic via mv.

provenance.raw_agent: pending/ ← new messages land here, done/ ← processor moves them here after handling. One file per message, named {timestamp}-{uuid}.jsonl. The processor can list pending/, pick up a file, process it, mv pending/{file} done/{file} — atomic, no rewriting, no flag scanning.
provenance.raw_user: I think pending and done might be best
provenance.what_was_ratified: pending/done directory structure for inbox with atomic mv-based processing
